summary: cursed-modules exfiltrates host data, env vars, and AWS credentials to hardcoded attacker endpoints on install and require
details: package.json wires preinstall, install, and postinstall to node install.js, and main is index.js whose top-level IIFE runs the same logic on require. Both scripts gate on a Docker-style hostname (^[0-9a-f]{12}$) then collect process.env, read filesystem paths (including /home/node, /app, /root), run aws sts get-caller-identity, curl the EC2 instance metadata service at 169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/ for IAM role credentials, and call aws secretsmanager list-secrets. Results are JSON-serialized, base64-encoded, wrapped in a YAML-looking 'ecto_module' manifest, and POSTed to http://154.57.164.82:30843/api/modules/ECT-... A second component, recon.js, reads installer-side secrets (/root/.npmrc, /home/user/.npmrc, Verdaccio configs and htpasswd, crontabs, /proc/1/environ, ps/netstat output) and PUTs base64/JSON payloads to http://154.57.164.76:30728/api/modules/ECT-654321 with a curl fallback. A shipped publish-and-arm.sh labels the campaign with ship_deck: 'dependency-confusion' and cargo_hold: 'verdaccio-proxy', and the package is published at version 999.0.2 — a high-version dependency-confusion pattern intended to outrank internal package versions in mixed registries. Installing or requiring this package in a container/CI environment immediately leaks environment variables, AWS IAM role credentials from IMDS, AWS Secrets Manager listings, and npm/Verdaccio registry tokens to attacker-controlled hosts.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
